Current Operations

    Other Operations

  • Homeland Security Investigations and the U.S. Coast Guard Seize $6.4 Million in Narcotics from Crude Oil Tanker in Port of Los Angeles DHS 22 May 2026 -- The United States Department of Homeland Security (DHS) released the following statement after U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement's (ICE) Homeland Security Investigations (HSI), the U.S. Coast Guard, and other federal agency partners conducted an operation that seized narcotics from a crude oil tanker in Los Angeles.

    • Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • 'Some Good Signs,' But Big 'Gaps' As Fitful US-Iran Negotiations Struggle On RFE/RL 22 May 2026 -- The state of US-Iran negotiations was in flux early on May 22, with the sides still at odds over future control of the Strait of Hormuz and Iran's right to enrich uranium -- among other issues -- and with the Pakistani mediator heading to Tehran in the latest peace push.

  • Dozens Of Iran-Linked Tankers In US Naval Blockade Area, Says Shipping Tracker RFE/RL 22 May 2026 -- TankerTrackers, a firm that monitors global oil shipments using satellite data, says that the United States Navy has halted a large number of vessels under US sanctions off the eastern coast of Oman

  • Satellite images reveal extent of damage to Israeli military bases in Iran's strikes Press TV 22 May 2026 -- Newly analyzed satellite imagery has exposed damage to multiple Israeli military installations from Iran and Hezbollah's defensive operations, raising questions about the extent of destruction that Tel Aviv may be concealing from public view.

  • Iran in talks with Oman on toll system for Hormuz Strait: Envoy Press TV 22 May 2026 -- The Iranian ambassador to France says the Islamic Republic and Oman have been discussing a permanent toll system for the Strait of Hormuz.
  • IRGC Navy coordinates safe passage of another 35 ships through Strait of Hormuz Press TV 22 May 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has announced that it coordinated the transit of another 35 ships through the Strait of Hormuz in the past 24 hours.
  • Iran's IRGC says 35 more ships cross Hormuz Strait in past 24 hrs in coordination with its navy Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 22 May 2026 -- The Navy of Iran's Islamic Revolution Guard Corps (IRGC) said Friday that 35 more vessels, including oil tankers, containerships and commercial vessels, have crossed the Strait of Hormuz within the past 24 hours in coordination with and under the protection of its forces, according to Xinhua.
  • Iran fires 3,300 missiles, drones at UAE since start of conflict Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 22 May 2026 -- Iran has launched nearly 3,300 missiles and drones at the United Arab Emirates since the start of the US and Israeli military operation against Tehran, Anwar Gargash, TASS reports citing diplomatic advisor to the UAE president.

  • Iran Talks See 'Slight Progress,' Says Rubio, Warns Against Toll For Hormuz Strait RFE/RL 22 May 2026 -- US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said there has been "slight progress" in Pakistan-mediated talks to end the Iran war, while warning against what he described as Tehran's attempt to establish a tolling system in the Strait of Hormuz, which he said no country should accept.
  • Trump says U.S. does not seek tolls in Strait of Hormuz People's Daily 22 May 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump said Thursday that the United States does not want to impose tolls on vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz, reversing remarks he made last month suggesting Washington would collect such tolls.
  • Chairman Wicker Issues Statement on Negotiations with Iran Senator Roger Wicker, R-Miss. 22 May 2026 -- "His instincts have been to finish the job he started in Iran, but he is being ill-advised to pursue a deal that would not be worth the paper it is written on. (...) We must finish what we started. It is past time for action."
  • US House Republicans Postpone War Powers Vote Likely To Go Against Trump RFE/RL 22 May 2026 -- Republican leaders of the US House of Representatives suddenly canceled a May 21 vote on a resolution aimed at forcing President Donald Trump to obtain authorization from Congress to continue the Iran war.
  • U.S. House Republican leaders cancel vote on limiting Trump's Iran war powers People's Daily 22 May 2026 -- U.S. House Republican leaders on Thursday canceled a scheduled vote on a resolution to limit President Donald Trump's war powers in Iran.

  • Iran says agreement with US not yet near as Pakistan continues mediation role Press TV 22 May 2026 -- The Foreign Ministry says Iran cannot yet say that an agreement with the United States is close, despite noting that trips made to Tehran by senior officials from Pakistan, which has been mediating talks between the two sides, point to arrival at a "decisive" juncture.
  • Baghaei appointed as spokesman for Iran's negotiating team with US Press TV 22 May 2026 -- The head of Iran's negotiating delegation in the talks with the United States has appointed Esmaeil Baghaei as the official spokesperson for the team.
  • Iranian Officials Meet With Qataris, But Stress Pakistan Remains Main Mediator RFE/RL 22 May 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baqaei on May 22 ⁠said a ⁠Qatari delegation was holding talks with Iran's foreign minister, but he added that Pakistan remains the main mediator ⁠in the negotiations between Washington and Tehran.

  • PM Sharif Heads To China As Pakistan Steps Up Iran Mediation Efforts RFE/RL 22 May 2026 -- Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif flies to Beijing on May 23 as his country seeks to reinvigorate its efforts to mediate in the ongoing standoff between the United States and Iran.
  • Pakistan army chief arrives in Tehran amid Iran-US mediation push Press TV 22 May 2026 -- Pakistan's army chief Field Marshal Asim Munir arrived in Tehran on Friday to discuss ongoing Iran-US negotiations aimed at ending the aggression that began when Washington and Tel Aviv launched military strikes on the Islamic Republic on February 28.

  • Senior UAE Official To RFE/RL: Restart Of Iran War Would Be 'Catastrophic' RFE/RL 22 May 2026 -- Anwar Gargash, a senior official in the United Arab Emirates, said a resumption of hostilities in the Iran war would be "catastrophic" for the Middle East, which has been embroiled in the US-Israeli conflict with the Islamic republic.
  • Senior UAE Official Warns Control Over Hormuz Would Set Dangerous Precedent, Sees '50-50' Chance Of US-Iran Deal RFE/RL 22 May 2026 -- Anwar Gargash, diplomatic adviser to the president of the United Arab Emirates, said any change in the status of the Strait of Hormuz would have serious consequences for the region and even Europe, warning that any form of control over the strategic waterway would create a dangerous precedent.
  • Middle East: Council extends EU legal framework to target those involved in Iran's actions impeding lawful transit passage and freedom of navigation EC/CEU 22 May 2026 -- The Council decided today to extend the scope of EU's restrictive measures originally established to address Tehran's military support for Russia's war of aggression against Ukraine and various armed groups in the Middle East and the Red Sea region.
  • EU Says Hormuz Strait Closure Against International Law RFE/RL 22 May 2026 -- Calling Iran's actions against vessels transiting through the Strait of Hormuz a violation of international law, the Council of the European Union has decided to impose restrictions on individuals and entities involved in these "actions and policies" by the Iranian authorities.
  • Japan-Iran Foreign Ministers' Telephone Call Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 22 May 2026
  • Yemen reaffirms support for Iran against any potential aggression Press TV 22 May 2026 -- The chairman of Yemen's Supreme Political Council has reiterated his country's full support for Iran, stressing that the recent events have showed that the Israeli regime can be defeated despite its military capabilities.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Department of War Publishes Second Release of Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena Files on WAR.GOV/UFO DoD 22 May 2026 -- Statement Attributable to Assistant to the Secretary of War for Public Affairs and Chief Pentagon Spokesman Sean Parnell:
  • 52d ADA BDE assesses IonStrike interceptors to support EFDI Army News 22 May 2026 -- 52d Air Defense Artillery Brigade (52d ADA BDE) is testing and evaluating new counter-Unmanned Aircraft Systems (c-UAS) capabilities, including IonStrike, a radar-agnostic, low-cost kinetic interceptor designed to strengthen layered defense against Unmanned Aircraft Systems (UAS).
  • Trump says U.S. to send 5,000 more troops to Poland after Pentagon's delay People's Daily 22 May 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump said Thursday his administration will send an additional 5,000 troops to Poland.
  • US Sending Additional 5,000 Troops to Poland, Trump Says Sputnik 22 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump announced on Thursday that additional 5,000 US troops will be deployed to Poland.
  • Readout of US Air Force Chief of Staff Gen. Ken Wilsbach's counterpart visit with Argentine Air Force Chief of Staff Brig. Gen. Gustavo Valverde US Air Force 22 May 2026
  • U.S. Coast Guard Cutter Waesche returns to Alameda after successful 113-day patrol in Bering Sea US Coast Guard 22 May 2026 -- The crew of U.S. Coast Guard Cutter Waesche (WMSL 751) returned to their homeport in Alameda Friday after concluding a 113-day patrol in the Bering Sea. The cutter traveled 18,685 nautical miles conducting a broad range of operations including maritime law enforcement, search and rescue and the integration of new capabilities, all while projecting U.S. presence in the high north environment.

  • Rubio Defends US Troop Decisions, Chides NATO At Foreign Ministers' Meeting RFE/RL 22 May 2026 -- HELSINGBORG, Sweden -- US Secretary of State Marco Rubio defended how Washington shifted military forces around the world amid confusion over whether a brigade-size contingent of US troops would be rotated out of Poland.
  • Secretary Rubio's Meeting with NATO Secretary General Rutte US Dept. of State 22 May 2026
  • Secretary of State Marco Rubio and NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte After Their Meeting US Dept. of State 22 May 2026
  • NATO Special Operations Forces strengthen Alliance Readiness through TROJAN FOOTPRINT 2026 NATO SHAPE 22 May 2026 -- TROJAN FOOTPRINT 2026 (TFP26), the largest iteration of the exercise to date, concluded on Thursday, May 21, 2026, after ten days of intensive multinational Special Operations Forces training across Europe.
  • NATO Foreign Ministers meeting in Sweden address how to make the Alliance stronger NATO 22 May 2026 -- NATO Foreign Ministers concluded a meeting in Helsingborg, Sweden, on Friday (22 May 2026). Their discussions helped lay the groundwork for the upcoming NATO Summit in Ankara this July.

  • Over 13,300 cases of russia's use of chemical agents on the battlefield have been officially documented Ukraine MOD 22 May 2026 -- The use of prohibited chemical warfare agents has become a systematic battlefield tactic employed by the russian army. Since the start of the full-scale invasion, more than 13,300 cases involving the use of chemical munitions by the enemy have been officially documented.

  • No update on U.S. arms sales to Taiwan: Presidential spokesperson CNA 22 May 2026 -- Taiwan has not received any information about possible adjustments to a potential US$14 billion arms sale package that U.S. President Donald Trump is reportedly considering, a spokesperson for Taiwan's Presidential Office said Friday.
  • US postpones arms sales to Taiwan as war on Iran depletes arsenal Press TV 22 May 2026 -- The United States has postponed the sales delivery of weapons and munitions to Chinese Taipei due to its arms' stocks depletion.

  • Major milestone for sovereign defence capability Australia DOD 22 May 2026 -- A new facility has opened at RAAF Base Edinburgh, South Australia, which is set to become a regional hub for the maintenance and sustainment of aircraft for the ADF and its partners.

  • Navy uncrewed vessels monitor Northland waters for narcotics trafficking New Zealand Defence Force 22 May 2026 -- The Royal New Zealand Navy's (RNZN) two Uncrewed Surface Vessels (USVs) have just completed the second of two border security operations for Customs, monitoring marine traffic in Northland.

  • New space system will better protect UK satellites as new imagery released UK MOD 22 May 2026 -- Vital emergency services, military operations and British businesses will be better protected through a new system to protect UK satellites and the services they provide from space.

  • Successful test-launch of Agni-1 Ballistic Missile India PIB 22 May 2026 -- Short Range Ballistic Missile 'Agni-1' was successfully test-launched from the Integrated Test Range at Chandipur, Odisha on May 22, 2026. The launch validated all operational and technical parameters. The test was carried out under the aegis of the Strategic Forces Command.

  • Decision on negotiations with France on Luleå-class frigates for air defence role Government Offices of Sweden 22 May 2026 -- The Government has recently tasked the Swedish Defence Materiel Administration with initiating negotiations on the procurement of four frigates based on the FDI concept (Frégate de Défense et d'Intervention) from France's Naval Group. The four frigates, which will be part of Sweden's Luleå class programme, are scheduled for delivery between 2030 and 2034.

  • European Peace Facility: Council adopts the third bilateral assistance measure in support of the Albanian Armed Forces EC/CEU 22 May 2026 -- The Council today adopted the third bilateral assistance measure under the European Peace Facility of €21 million with the aim of strengthening defence capacities and capabilities of the Albanian Armed Forces, bringing the total amount of support to Albania to €49 million to date.

  • EU-Azerbaijan: Joint Press Release on the 7th Security Dialogue in Baku European External Action Service (EEAS) 22 May 2026 -- On 20 May, Azerbaijan and the European Union held the seventh meeting of their Security Dialogue in Baku.

  • Baiba Braže at the meeting of NATO Ministers of Foreign Affairs: A united, strong NATO is the best response to threats from Russia and others; Europe must do more for defence Latvia MFA 22 May 2026 -- At the meeting of NATO Ministers of Foreign Affairs held on 21 and 22 May 2026 in Helsingborg, Sweden, the main focus was on the priorities of the NATO Summit in Ankara scheduled for 7 and 8 July.

  • NATO fighter aircraft in Šiauliai are a sign of Allied units, said R. Kaunas to Minister of National Defence of Romania Lithuania MND 22 May 2026 -- Today, Minister of Defence of Lithuania Robertas Kaunas met with Romanian counterpart Radu-Dinel Miruță at the Lithuanian Air Force Base in Šiauliai. They focused on the incidents of the recent weeks caused by drone breeches into the Baltic airspace.

  • Tsahkna at NATO foreign ministers' meeting: Europe will continue taking greater responsibility for our shared security Estonia MFA 22 May 2026 -- Today, 22 May, at the meeting of NATO foreign ministers in Helsingborg, Foreign Minister Margus Tsahkna said that the drone incident in Estonia demonstrates that NATO is functioning effectively and that Europe will continue taking greater responsibility for our shared security.
  • Estonia and the United Kingdom reaffirm close defence cooperation Estonia MFA 22 May 2026 -- The Estonian Minister of Defence, Hanno Pevkur, met in Tallinn with the UK Secretary of State for Defence, John Healey, to discuss long-term bilateral defence cooperation and its strategic expansion.

  • Defence spending, support for Ukraine top agenda of NATO ministerial meeting Government of Iceland 22 May 2026 -- The transatlantic relationship, increased defence spending, more equitable burden-sharing among allies and continued support for Ukraine were among the main topics at the NATO foreign ministers meeting that concluded in Helsingborg, Sweden, today. Þorgerður Katrín Gunnarsdóttir, Minister for Foreign Affairs, participated in the meeting on Iceland´s behalf.

  • ETH Domain strengthens existing cooperation with the DDPS and armasuisse Federal Council - Swiss Government 22 May 2026 -- At its meeting of 20/21 May, the ETH Board discussed the conditions for the cooperation with the DDPS and the Federal Office for Defence Procurement, armasuisse, which is to be intensified in accordance with the Federal Council's new armaments policy strategy.
  • Research Program Security: The DDPS and the ETH domain are strengthening research in the area of security and defence Federal Council - Swiss Government 22 May 2026 -- The Federal Office for Defence Procurement armasuisse and the ETH domain are strengthening their strategic cooperation in security-relevant research.

  • Deputy Prime Minister Radosław Sikorski takes part in NATO foreign ministers meeting Poland - Ministry of Foreign Affairs 22 May 2026 -- Transatlantic relations and increased defence spending were the focus of a NATO foreign ministers meeting on 21-22 May with Deputy Prime Minister Radosław Sikorski among the participants. Gathered in Helsingborg, Sweden, the top diplomats also spoke about preparations for the upcoming NATO Summit in Ankara.

  • Albares calls for unity and cohesion within the Alliance and urges a return to negotiations and an end to hostilities in the Middle East Spain MFA 22 May 2026 -- The minister emphasises that a stronger Europe strengthens NATO and reinforces the transatlantic bond

  • Manila hosts high-level Philippines-European Union seminar on maritime crisis response followed by Exercise Alalayan 2026 European External Action Service (EEAS) 22 May 2026 -- The European Union (EU), in collaboration with the Philippine National Maritime Center, has organized a high-level seminar in Manila on coordinating responses to large-scale maritime crises, followed by the annual, multi-agency maritime security drill Exercise Alalayan.
  • Dutch warship De Ruyter in PH for maritime partnership activities Philippine News Agency 22 May 2026 -- Dutch Navy warship HNLMS De Ruyter is in Manila for a three-day goodwill visit as part of the Netherlands' intensified efforts to strengthen maritime partnerships with countries in the Indo-Pacific.

  • Trump rules out use of force to take Greenland: U.S. ambassador People's Daily 22 May 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump has ruled out using military force to take control of Greenland, U.S. Ambassador to Denmark Ken Howery said on Thursday during the opening of a new U.S. consulate building in Nuuk, the capital of Greenland.

  • Secretary of State Marco Rubio Remarks to Press US Dept. of State 22 May 2026
  • Joint Statement on Arctic Security from the Arctic Allies - Canada, Kingdom of Denmark including Greenland and the Faroe Islands, Finland, Iceland, Norway, Sweden, and the United States of America US Dept. of State 22 May 2026 -- The following is the text of a joint statement by the Arctic Allies: Canada, Kingdom of Denmark including Greenland and the Faroe Islands, Finland, Iceland, Norway, Sweden, and the United States of America.
  • Secretary Rubio's Meeting with Prime Minister Kristersson US Dept. of State 22 May 2026
  • Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Swedish Foreign Minister Maria Malmer Stenergard at the United States-Sweden Technology Prosperity Deal Signing Ceremony US Dept. of State 22 May 2026

  • 22 May 2026 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 22 May 2026

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots | Roaring Lion (Lebanon)

  • Israeli strikes kill six paramedics in south Lebanon Press TV 22 May 2026 -- Lebanon's Health Ministry says six paramedics and a child have been killed in Israeli strikes on Southern Lebanon despite a ceasefire agreement officially in effect.

  • Gaza: Life-saving medicines blocked as killing continues, disease gains ground UN News 22 May 2026 -- Dire conditions in Gaza marked by continuing violence, rodent infestations and the spread of infectious disease are being made worse by blockages of essential medical supplies, UN agencies warned on Friday.
  • Rooted for Lebanon: Launch of International Humanitarian Alliance to Support People of Lebanon IOM 22 May 2026 -- An alliance of international and local humanitarian non-governmental organizations in Lebanon have launched a global fundraising campaign to help families across the country recover from the compounded impacts of conflict, displacement and economic crisis. The initiative is led under the auspices of the Lebanese Ministry of Social Affairs and facilitated by the International Organization for Migration (IOM).

  • Joint statement on the situation in the West Bank. Élysée - President of France 22 May 2026 -- "Over the past few months, the situation in the West Bank has deteriorated significantly. Settler violence is at unprecedented levels. The policies and practices of the Israeli government, including a further entrenchment of Israeli control, are undermining stability and prospects for a two-state solution."

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Democratic Republic of the Congo, Lebanon, Occupied Palestinian Territory, Nigeria, Bangladesh, Colombia UNOCHA 22 May 2026 -- UN Relief Chief boosts Ebola response with up to $60 million allocation

  • Türk: States must halt involuntary returns to Afghanistan OHCHR 22 May 2026 -- UN High Commissioner for Human Rights Volker Türk on Friday warned against the continuing trend of involuntary returns of Afghan refugees and asylum-seekers from host countries to Afghanistan, in violation of international human rights and refugee law.

  • Statement of Deputy Prosecutor Nazhat Shameem Khan to the United Nations Security Council on the Situation in Libya, pursuant to Resolution 1970 (2011) ICC 22 May 2026 -- On 22 May 2026, ICC Deputy Prosecutor Nazhat Shameem Khan addressed the United Nations Security Council on the Situation in Libya, remotely from The Hague. Below is a transcript of her remarks
  • Accountability remains important to ensuring stability in Libya: UK statement at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 22 May 2026 -- Statement by Legal Advisor Colin McIntyre at the UN Security Council meeting on Libya.
  • Some Measure of Justice Under Way in Libya as International Criminal Court's First Trial of Major War Criminal Suspect Begins, Deputy Prosecutor Tells Security Council UN Security Council 22 May 2026 -- Briefing the Security Council on the International Criminal Court's work in Libya, its Deputy Prosecutor today said recent judicial proceedings, including the confirmation hearing of Khaled Mohamed Ali El Hishri, have brought victims "some measure of justice, of healing", while urging stronger cooperation to secure arrests and advance accountability for war crimes, crimes against humanity and abuses against migrants.

  • Stabilization Gains Open Pathway to Development in Central African Republic: IOM Chief of Staff IOM 22 May 2026 -- The Central African Republic's stabilization gains are opening a critical pathway toward long-term recovery and development, which the international community must continue to consolidate through investments in human capital development and livelihoods.

  • Joint Statement from the International Contact Group for the Great Lakes (ICG) on the Situation in the Eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) US Dept. of State 22 May 2026 -- The text of the following statement was released by the International Contact Group (ICG) for the Great Lakes, including representatives of Belgium, Denmark, the European Union, France, Germany, the Netherlands, Sweden, Switzerland, the United Kingdom and the United States following their meeting on May 20-21, 2026.

News Reports

  • Trump's top spy resigns under White House pressure over illegal war on Iran Press TV 22 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump's Director of National Intelligence (DNI) Tulsi Gabbard has resigned amid intense pressure from the White House after she strongly objected to the US-led illegal war of aggression against Iran, informed sources have revealed.
  • Chairman Crawford on the Resignation of Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard US House of Representatives Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence 22 May 2026 -- Today, House Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence Chairman Rick Crawford (AR-01) issued the following statement on the resignation of Director Gabbard as the Director of National Intelligence.
  • Statement of Senate Intel Vice Chair Warner on the Resignation of Tulsi Gabbard as DNI Office of Senator Mark R. Warner (D-VA) 22 May 2026

  • Russia's unfounded allegations: EP leaders express full solidarity with the Baltic states European External Action Service (EEAS) 22 May 2026 -- Statement of the European Parliament's Conference of Presidents in response to Russia's allegations against Latvia as well as Estonia and Lithuania.
  • Statement of the Ministers of Foreign Affairs of the Nordic- Baltic countries on Russia's latest disinformation campaign Estonia MFA 22 May 2026 -- "The Ministers of Foreign Affairs of Nordic-Baltic countries firmly reject Russia's blatant disinformation campaign and false claims, supported by Belarus, following the airspace violations in the Nordic-Baltic region."
  • Nordic and Baltic foreign ministers condemn Russian disinformation campaign in joint statement Government of Norway 22 May 2026 -- Today, 22 May, the foreign ministers of the Nordic and Baltic countries issued a joint statement strongly condemning the disinformation campaign and false claims by Russia and Belarus concerning airspace violations in the Nordic-Baltic region.

  • Ebola risk 'very high' in eastern DR Congo as UN intensifies response UN News 22 May 2026 -- The UN is rushing emergency personnel, funding and supplies into eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) to counter the fast-growing Ebola outbreak spreading through conflict-ravaged provinces.
  • UN Relief Chief boosts Ebola response with up to US$60 million allocation UNOCHA 22 May 2026 -- Statement by Tom Fletcher, Under-Secretary-General for Humanitarian Affairs and Emergency Relief Coordinator
  • WFP scales up emergency Ebola response in eastern DRC to contain risk of regional crisis WFP 22 May 2026 -- The United Nations World Food Programme (WFP) is urgently scaling up its emergency response in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), working with the Government, the World Health Organization (WHO) and partners to help contain the Ebola outbreak before a health emergency transforms into a broader humanitarian catastrophe.
  • First meeting of the IHR Emergency Committee regarding the epidemic of Ebola Bundibugyo virus disease in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda 2026 - Temporary recommendations WHO 22 May 2026 -- On 17 May 2026, pursuant to paragraph 2 of Article 12 - Determination of a public health emergency of international concern, including a pandemic emergency of the International Health Regulations (2005) (IHR), the Director-General (DG) of the World Health Organization (WHO), after having consulted the States Parties where the event was known to be occurring, determined that the epidemic of Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda constitutes a public health emergency of international concern (PHEIC), but did not meet the criteria of pandemic emergency, as defined in the IHR.
  • Norway contributes NOK 50 million to the WHO Health Contingencies Fund following Ebola outbreak Government of Norway 22 May 2026 -- Norway is strengthening the World Health Organization's (WHO) Health Contingencies Fund with a contribution of NOK 50 million following a new outbreak of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DR Congo) and Uganda.

  • Members of Transnational Money Laundering Organization Charged with Laundering Cartel Funds US Dept. of Justice 22 May 2026 -- An indictment returned in the Eastern District of Virginia was unsealed yesterday charging Ruhuan Zhen and Hongce Wu, both Chinese nationals, with conspiracy to commit money laundering in connection with transnational criminal organizations, including the Sinaloa Cartel and the Cartel de Jalisco Nueva Generación (CJNG).

  • No: 99, 22 May 2026, Regarding the Release of Alexandros Giotopoulos, Convicted Leader of the 17 November Terrorist Organization Republic of Turkey Ministry of Foreign Affairs 22 May 2026

  • NASA to Compete Contract for Jet Propulsion Laboratory Management NASA 22 May 2026 -- NASA announced plans Friday to compete the next contract for managing and operating the agency's federally funded research and development center (FFRDC) in Southern California at the Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL), to ensure continued accountability and strong value for U.S. taxpayers.

  • Meeting with permanent members of the Security Council President of Russia 22 May 2026 -- The President held a briefing session, via videoconference, with permanent members of the Security Council.

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun's Regular Press Conference on May 22, 2026 PRC MofA 22 May 2026
  • Xi-Putin meeting charts new strategic course for China-Russia ties PRC MND 22 May 2026 -- The high-profile meeting between Chinese President Xi Jinping and Russian President Vladimir Putin in Beijing on Wednesday set the course for the next stage of bilateral relations and underscored the two major countries' commitment to advancing global governance reform in the right direction.

  • Taiwan, Europe should deepen strategic partnership: Foreign minister CNA 22 May 2026 -- Foreign Minister Lin Chia-lung (林佳龍) on Thursday called for Taiwan and Europe to deepen strategic cooperation, saying Taiwan could become a key partner in Europe's reindustrialization and rearmament efforts amid rising geopolitical tensions and rapid advances in artificial intelligence (AI).
  • China's opposition to US arms sales to Taiwan region consistent, clear and firm, says FM after acting US navy secretary says Washington paused arms sale to island Global Times 22 May 2026 -- China's opposition to US arms sales to China's Taiwan region is consistent, clear and firm, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Friday at a regular press briefing, when asked to comment on reports that the acting US navy secretary said Thursday that arm sales to Taiwan had been put on pause to ensure that the American military had sufficient munitions for its Iran operations, and that Taiwan authorities claimed on Friday that there was no information indicating that the US intends to make any adjustments to this arms sale.
  • Lai's anniversary speech slammed for covering up failure, advocating secessionism People's Daily 22 May 2026 -- As Taiwan leader Lai Ching-te's tenure reached the two-year mark on Wednesday, his anniversary speech triggered a wave of criticism, with various sectors in Taiwan accusing him of covering up poor performance and deepening cross-Strait tensions.
  • Shaheen, Tillis, Coons, Collins Introduce Bipartisan Resolution Reaffirming U.S. Support for Taiwan US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 22 May 2026 -- Today, U.S. Senators Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, and Thom Tillis (R-NC), Co-Chair of the Senate Taiwan Caucus, introduced a bipartisan resolution reaffirming the Taiwan Relations Act of 1979 as well as longstanding bipartisan support for the U.S.-Taiwan partnership. Senators Shaheen and Tillis were joined by Senators Chris Coons (D-DE) and Susan Collins (R-ME) in cosponsoring the resolution.
  • Supreme Court upholds sentence for ex-Air Force officer in China leak case CNA 22 May 2026 -- The Supreme Court has upheld a seven-year jail sentence for an ex-Air Force officer found guilty of leaking military documents to China in exchange for money.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 22 May 2026

  • Exclusive: US Asks Lithuania, Poland, Ukraine To Allow Belarusian Fertilizer Exports RFE/RL 22 May 2026 -- US officials have proposed that Lithuania, Poland, and Ukraine lift sanctions on Belarusian potash to allow the transit of the agricultural product - a major source of hard currency revenue for Minsk --- through their territories.

  • Trump says he will be the one to intervene in Cuba People's Daily 22 May 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump said Thursday his predecessors have mulled intervening in Cuba for decades and now it looks like he will be the one that does it.

  • Transcript of Special Briefing by MEA on the State Visit of President of Cyprus to India (May 22, 2026) India MEA 22 May 2026

  • Transcript of the Press Briefing by the Spokesperson on Friday 22nd May 2026 Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 22 May 2026

  • The Daily Press Briefing (05/22/2026) France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 22 May 2026

  • Press conference - Jervis Bay Prime Minister of Australia 22 May 2026
  • Press conference - Wollongong Prime Minister of Australia 22 May 2026

  • Secretary of State Sarai strengthens global partnerships while in United Kingdom and Nigeria Canada 22 May 2026 -- The Honourable Randeep Sarai, Secretary of State (International Development), has concluded a visit to the United Kingdom and Nigeria, from May 18 to 22, 2026, to reinforce Canada's commitment to modernizing development partnerships, strengthening collaboration with key allies and advancing inclusive economic opportunities through deeper engagement with African partners.

  • China firmly opposes US' deployment of mid-range missile systems in Asian countries, urges US and Japan to heed calls from regional countries, rectify erroneous moves: FM Global Times 22 May 2026 -- When asked to comment on reports that the US is planning to deploy the Typhon mid-range missile system at Kanoya Air Base in Kagoshima Prefecture, Japan, from June to September for joint military drills, and relocate the system to US military bases in Japan after the exercises, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Friday that China has noted the relevant reports and firmly opposes the deployment of mid-range missile systems by the US in Asian countries and had repeatedly voiced concerns over the issue.
  • Japan peeling away 'peaceful nation' disguise, pushing toward neo-militarism: Chinese FM on LDP's request on rising defense spending Global Times 22 May 2026 -- The latest data shows that Japan's defense spending in 2025 increased by 9.7 percent, with both the total amount and its share of GDP reaching record highs, while arms imports surged 76 percent over the past five years. Despite defense spending having risen for 14 consecutive years, right-wing forces in Japan are still clamoring for further increases in military expenditure. This once again shows that Japan is peeling away the disguise of its so-called "peaceful nation" layer by layer and steadily moving down the wrong path of "neo-militarism," Guo Jiakun, a spokesperson for the Chinese Foreign Ministry, said at a regular press conference on Friday.
  • Japan-Albania Summit Meeting Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 22 May 2026
  • Japan-Laos Foreign Ministers' Meeting Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 22 May 2026

  • Saudi Vision 2030 in freefall as fiscal woes from US-Israeli war on Iran deepens Press TV 22 May 2026 -- Saudi Arabia has halted new contracts for Western consultancies and delayed payments as the kingdom struggles with a ballooning budget deficit triggered by the US-Israeli aggression against Iran and its regional fallout, exposing the fragility of Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman's flagship Vision 2030 program.

  • Türkiye: Statement by the Spokesperson on the decision by the 36th Ankara Regional Court of Justice European External Action Service (EEAS) 22 May 2026 -- As an EU candidate country and a long-standing member of the Council of Europe, Türkiye is expected to uphold the highest democratic standards and practices. Democracy, the rule of law and respect for fundamental rights are central to the EU accession process.
  • Secretary Rubio's Meeting with Turkish Foreign Minister Fidan US Dept. of State 22 May 2026

  • Diaspora Remittances Point to Untapped Potential in Crisis Response: New IOM Report IOM 22 May 2026 -- As diaspora remittances now outpace both official development assistance and foreign direct investment combined, the International Organization for Migration (IOM) today published a new paper showing how stronger engagement with diaspora communities can enhance humanitarian response and support recovery efforts worldwide.
